goal: Design a GitLab CI pipeline that hard-gates USD/URDF PRs via headless Isaac Sim validation.
thoughts: Hard gates = non-negotiable fail conditions: unresolved refs, negative mass, NaN transform, contact-force explosion at Play, broken articulation. Soft-advisory for perf regressions. Diff-based selectivity is critical — 90 s scene load x N scenes is unusable; only validate scenes touched in the MR. Per-runner GPU allocation: headless Isaac Sim needs EGL, so runners must be GPU-tagged. Un-game-able checks: numeric physics health, mass > 0, ref resolution — these can't be worked around by engineers. Never propose GUI validation.
code:
# 1. Validation script invoked per changed scene (runs inside isaac-sim container)
# validate_scene.py (called by .gitlab-ci.yml)
preflight = preflight_check(scene_path="<CHANGED_SCENE>")
import_ok = verify_import(prim_path="<ROBOT_PRIM>")
analysis = run_stage_analysis(scene_path="<CHANGED_SCENE>", checks=["unresolved_refs", "negative_mass", "nan_transform"])
physics = check_physics_health(focus="contact_force_explosion", play_duration_s=2.0)
meshes = check_collision_mesh(scene_path="<CHANGED_SCENE>")

failed = any(not x.ok for x in [preflight, import_ok, analysis, physics, meshes])
if failed:
    export_scene_package(source_scene="<CHANGED_SCENE>", dest="/artifacts/failure_bundle.usdz")
    raise SystemExit(1)

# 2. .gitlab-ci.yml fragment — diff-based selectivity
# stages: [validate]
# validate_usd:
#   image: <ISAAC_SIM_HEADLESS_IMAGE_DIGEST>
#   tags: [gpu, isaac-sim]  # runners with GPU
#   rules:
#     - changes: ["**/*.usd", "**/*.usda", "**/*.urdf"]
#   script:
#     - |
#       for f in $(git diff --name-only $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_DIFF_BASE_SHA HEAD -- '*.usd' '*.urdf'); do
#         ./omni.isaac.sim.headless.sh --exec validate_scene.py --scene $f
#       done
#   artifacts:
#     when: on_failure
#     paths: [artifacts/]

# 3. Scene load caching: bake a warm-cache image per week; PR runs inherit the cache
failure_modes: assistant proposes re-running full scene suite per PR
assistant doesn't tag GPU runners for headless EGL
